name: Kefallinian Spicy Meat Pie
recipeIngredient: 1 small Leg of lamb (boned and cut into 1-inch pieces)
3 medium Potatoes, parboiled in their jackets, peeled and diced
Orange or lemon peel, cut into pieces
recipeInstructions:
On the island of Kefallinia in the Ionion Sea, the Feast Day of Analipseos (Ascension Day) is celebrated with the traditional "kreatopita" (meat-pie). This spicy pie also ushers in the beginning of Lent on the day of Apokreas.
1. In a stock pot, cover lamb bones with cold water. Simmer, covered for 1 hour. Strain, boil down to 1 cup, and set aside. Sprinkle lemon juice on lamb cubes.
2. Heat oil or butter in a heavy pan. Add onions and lamb, sautéing meat until all sides are browned and onions soft. Pour onions, lamb, and juices into a large bowl.
3. Add diced potatoes, carrot, rice, tomato puree, cheese, parsley, mint, oregano, garlic, cinnamon, and fruit peel to the bowl. Season with salt and pepper.
4. Add enough reserved lamb broth for liquid while pie bakes. Mix well with a wooden spoon.
5. Butter bottom and sides of 9 x 12 x 3 inch pan. Spread 8 filo sheets, brushing melted butter between each sheet. Ensure pastry fits sides and bottom.
6. Pour filling into the pan, spreading evenly with spatula. Place egg quarters across top and cover with remaining filo sheets. Brush with butter as before.
7. Flute edges with fingers or fork. Brush top with butter. Score top 8 filo sheets into squares or diamonds using a sharp knife or by pricking with a fork.
8. Bake for 40 to 50 minutes at moderately slow oven (325 F), then raise temperature to 350 F for last 10 minutes.
9. Remove from oven and let stand on rack for 15 minutes. Cut into diamonds or squares and serve warm.
